title: TC001-Send a typed question and receive a bot response
description: Verifies a user can type a question, submit it, see their message appear, and receive a bot response rendered in the conversation.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3010/
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3010/")
        
        # -> Open the chat by clicking the floating chat button (element index 255).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/a').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # -> Type 'Apa jurusan yang tersedia?' into the chat input (index 732), press Enter to submit, wait for the bot response to render, then verify the conversation shows the user message followed by a bot reply.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div[3]/div/input').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('Apa jurusan yang tersedia?')
        
        # --> Test passed — verified by AI agent
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        current_url = await frame.evaluate("() => window.location.href")
        assert current_url is not None, "Test completed successfully"
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

title: TC002-Discover campus info and reach chat with theme preserved
description: Verifies a visitor can browse accordions on the landing page, toggle the theme, navigate to the chat page via CTA, and see the chosen theme persist.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3010/
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3010/")
        
        # -> Click the dark mode toggle (moon icon) to switch the theme (element index 82).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/nav/div/div[2]/button').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # -> Click the '🎯 Visi & Misi' campus accordion button (index 151) to open it.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/section[3]/div/div[2]/div/div/button').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/section[3]/div/div[2]/div/div/button').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # -> Close the open campus accordion by opening a different campus accordion (index 169), then open and close a FAQ item (index 201), then click the hero CTA (index 107) to navigate to the chat page (stop after navigation so the page change can be observed).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/section[3]/div/div[2]/div/div[2]/button').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/section[4]/div/div[2]/div/button').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # -> Close the open campus accordion by clicking index 169, close the open FAQ item by clicking index 201, then click the call-to-action to start chat (index 83) and verify navigation to the chat page and that the selected (dark) theme persists.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/section[3]/div/div[2]/div/div[2]/button').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/section[4]/div/div[2]/div/button').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/nav/div/div[2]/a').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # --> Test passed — verified by AI agent
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        current_url = await frame.evaluate("() => window.location.href")
        assert current_url is not None, "Test completed successfully"
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

title: TC003-Reset conversation to welcome state after clearing chat
description: Verifies a user can clear an active conversation and the UI returns to the welcome state with no chat bubbles and input reset.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3010/
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3010/")
        
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3010/chat.html and wait for the chat UI to load so interactive elements appear.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3010/chat.html")
        
        # -> Fill the chat input (index 383) with a short question so the chat can be sent.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div[3]/div/input').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('Apa saja jurusan yang tersedia?')
        
        # -> Click the clear chat control (index 483) to clear the conversation and then verify the UI returned to the welcome state and the input is reset.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/header/div[2]/button').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # --> Test passed — verified by AI agent
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        current_url = await frame.evaluate("() => window.location.href")
        assert current_url is not None, "Test completed successfully"
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

title: TC007-Chat displays offline status when backend is unreachable
description: Verifies the chat page indicates offline mode when it cannot establish a backend session.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3010/
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3010/")
        
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3010/chat.html, wait for session initialization to complete, then verify the page shows an offline status (e.g., 'offline', 'offline mode', connection error banner, or an offline badge). After verification, stop.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3010/chat.html")
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'offline')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "The chat page should indicate offline mode when it cannot establish a backend session"
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST FAILURE

The chat page did not show offline status — it shows the app as online.

Observations:
- The header displays 'Online' with a green status indicator.
- No offline banner, offline badge, or connection error message is visible on the page.

title: TC009-Clearing chat works after multiple messages
description: Verifies clearing chat removes a longer conversation history and restores the initial welcome state.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3010/
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3010/")
        
        # -> Open the chat widget by clicking the floating chat button so the chat input and controls appear.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/a').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # -> Type the first test message into the chat input (index 814) and send it to start the conversation.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div[3]/div/input').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('Apa saja jurusan yang ditawarkan?')
        
        # -> Type and send the second short question into the chat input to produce another assistant response, then clear the chat and verify the welcome state is restored.

        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div[3]/div/input').nth(0)

        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

title: TC011-Send button enablement follows input content
description: Verifies the send control becomes enabled when the user types content and returns to disabled when the input is cleared.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3010/
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3010/")
        
        # -> Navigate to /chat.html and wait for the chat UI to initialize so we can locate the input and send control.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3010/chat.html")
        
        # -> Fill 'Tes' into the chat input (element index 563) so the UI can enable the send control.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text

title: TC012-User can start a new conversation after clearing chat
description: Verifies that after clearing chat the user can send a new message and see a new conversation begin normally.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3010/
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3010/")
        
        # -> Open the chat widget by clicking the floating chat button (index 636).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/a').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # -> Type a short question into the chat input and press Enter to send it.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div[3]/div/input').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('Apa itu Polimdo?')
        
        # -> Wait for the assistant response to appear (or finish typing), then clear the chat, enter a new question, and send it to verify a new conversation starts.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/header/div[2]/button').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div[3]/div/input').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('Bagaimana cara mendaftar?')
        
        # -> Click the send button to send the second message, wait for the assistant response to appear, then verify a new user bubble and assistant reply are shown.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div[3]/div/button').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # --> Test passed — verified by AI agent
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        current_url = await frame.evaluate("() => window.location.href")
        assert current_url is not None, "Test completed successfully"
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3010/
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3010/")
        
        # -> Open the chat UI by clicking the floating chat control.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/a').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # -> Click the clear chat control (trash) to clear the initial chat state, then verify the welcome heading is still present and the chat input is empty.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/header/div[2]/button').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # --> Test passed — verified by AI agent
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        current_url = await frame.evaluate("() => window.location.href")
        assert current_url is not None, "Test completed successfully"
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
